nameMeanings.name: Broghe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: topographic name derived from broc, an Old Occitan word (of ultimate Gaulish origin; see 2 below) denoting a ‘rocky spur’ or ‘place with a spiny vegetation’ (compare Dubroc 1).
metonymic occupational name from an Old French and Old Occitan derivative of Latin broccum (from Gaulish broccos), denoting different pointed objects such as needle and spit.

variants.name: Brochu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Aime, Fernand, Emile, Laurent, Marcel, Cecile, Donat, Gaetan, Gilles, Jacques.
French: occupational name from an Old French derivative of Latin broccum, denoting different pointed objects such as needle and spit (compare Broche ).
History: Jean Brochu from Montaigu in Vendée, France, married Nicole Saulnier in Sainte-Famille, Île d'Orléans, QC, in 1669.

variants.name: Dubroc
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camile, Florien, Gaston, Pierre.

topographic name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, from broc, an Old Occitan word denoting a ‘rocky spur’ or ‘place with a spiny vegetation’ (see Broche ), or a habitational name for someone from Le Broc, the name of several places in various parts of France, named with this word.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Broghe was born about 1547, in City of London, England, United Kingdom. She married Broghe about 1567. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.
